author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Truck Repair Shops Win on Google Ads With $2K Monthly Budgets
description: Stop burning Google Ads budget. We show truck repair shops exactly which keywords convert, bid strategies that work, and how to generate 8-12 qualified leads monthly without overspending.

sections.p: Every truck repair shop owner we work with says the same thing: "I ran Google Ads once and got killed by costs." The problem isn't Google Ads itself. It's that they're bidding on generic, high-cost keywords and not capturing trucks actively needing repair right now. We've helped 17 shops in our portfolio go from scattered $3K+ monthly spend to consistent $1,800-$2,200 budgets pulling 8-14 qualified calls per month. Here's the framework we use.

sections.h2: Stop Bidding on "Truck Repair" — Start Bidding on Repair Types

sections.p: The keyword "truck repair" in most markets runs $8-$15 per click. You'll burn through a $2K budget in 130-250 clicks with barely any conversions. Instead, we segment into repair-type keywords: "transmission repair truck" ($4-6 CPC), "diesel engine repair near me" ($5-7 CPC), "brake service heavy duty" ($3-5 CPC). These convert 3-4x better because someone searching "transmission repair truck" isn't just browsing—their transmission is slipping.

list: "Transmission repair heavy duty" — targets urgent, high-margin repairs
"Diesel truck maintenance" — catches preventive maintenance spending
"Suspension alignment truck" — captures fleet maintenance contracts
"Engine diagnostics truck repair" — brings shops looking for diagnosis, not just tire rotation
"Truck electrical repair" — low-volume, high-intent keyword in most markets

sections.h2: Geo-Target by Fleet Density, Not Just City Limits

sections.p: Most shops set location radius to "10 miles from my shop." That's too small. We expand to 20-25 miles, then layer in negative keywords for nearby competitors' neighborhoods if needed. Here's why: fleet operators and owner-operators often travel 30+ minutes to a shop they trust. A shop in suburban Denver can pull trucks from a 25-mile radius because drivers are willing to travel for quality. We've seen shops add 15-20% more qualified leads just by expanding radius and adding high-intent modifiers like "emergency truck repair" or "mobile truck service."

sections.quote: We started with $2,500/month blind-shotgun budget. After six weeks targeting repair types + fleet areas, we cut spend to $1,900 and got 11 calls last month instead of 4. The difference was intention—we're no longer chasing people casually searching 'nearby mechanic.'

sections.h2: Use Call Extensions and Lead Forms — Skip Website Clicks

sections.p: Your Google Ad should make calling you the easiest action, not landing on your website. Call extensions get clicked 10-15% more than website links. Better yet: add a lead form extension. Fleet managers and owner-operators will submit their number for a callback rather than dialing. We've set up lead forms for 9 shops asking just: truck type, repair needed, and phone number. Average conversion rate on the form itself is 18-22%, and those leads close at 35-40% because you can call them back immediately while they're thinking about repair.

sections.h2: Measure Cost Per Qualified Lead, Not Cost Per Click

sections.p: One shop was showing us a 3% click-to-call conversion rate and thought they were crushing it. That meant $0.15 CPC converted to $5 cost per actual phone call. We reframed their tracking: are those calls coming from fleet operators (high-value), one-off owner-operators (medium), or tire-kickers (low)? We added a simple tag in their phone system: when the shop answers, they note "fleet," "owner-op," or "one-off." Within 30 days, they could see that their "diesel engine repair" keyword was pulling fleet calls at $18 per lead, while "oil change truck" was pulling one-offs at $42 per lead. They killed the oil change ad and doubled down on engine work. Cost per qualified lead dropped 28%.
